combat_history.event: Unconfirmed reports of T-14 deployment near Ukrainian border for evaluation. No confirmed combat engagements documented.
combat_history.significance: Suggests Russia may have considered combat testing but likely held back due to capture/loss concerns

combat_history.event: Single T-14 broke down during rehearsal, replaced by T-34 for actual parade
combat_history.significance: Public demonstration of ongoing reliability issues affecting even ceremonial operations

modernization.description: Enhanced armor package, improved Afghanit APS, upgraded digital systems, and reliability improvements based on initial service experience
modernization.impact: Intended to address early production reliability issues and enhance protection against latest Western ATGMs

modernization.description: Resumed limited production after 2-year halt, focusing on resolving manufacturing and quality control issues
modernization.impact: Critical for achieving meaningful operational numbers and validating platform viability

vulnerabilities.description: Chronic manufacturing problems, component failures, and extremely limited production numbers. Only ~132 vehicles produced vs original 2,300 planned.
vulnerabilities.context: Western sanctions have disrupted supply chains for advanced electronics and materials needed for production
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Attempted shift to domestic components and simplified systems, but progress remains slow

vulnerabilities.description: No confirmed combat experience despite ongoing conflict in Ukraine. Complex systems remain untested under battlefield conditions.
vulnerabilities.context: Ukraine conflict has revealed significant shortcomings in Russian armor doctrine and crew training
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Limited field testing and evaluation, but reluctance to risk high-value platforms in combat

vulnerabilities.description: Advanced digital systems and unique design require specialized maintenance and training that Russian forces may lack at scale
vulnerabilities.context: Russian military has struggled with maintaining less complex systems during current operations
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Simplified maintenance protocols and enhanced crew training programs, effectiveness uncertain

vulnerabilities.description: Estimated $8M per unit cost is 4-5x higher than upgraded T-72/T-80 variants, making mass procurement unlikely
vulnerabilities.context: Russia's defense budget is under pressure from sanctions and ongoing military operations
vulnerabilities.mitigation: Focus on T-72/T-80 upgrades for bulk forces while T-14 remains limited to elite units

operational_patterns.typical_deployment: Limited to evaluation units and elite formations; primarily used for testing and training
operational_patterns.typical_task_group: Small platoons or companies for evaluation, not integrated into standard tank battalions
operational_patterns.readiness_notes: Low operational readiness due to maintenance issues and spare parts shortages

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Abrams emphasizes proven reliability and extensive upgrade path, while T-14 features more radical design changes but unproven performance. Abrams has extensive combat history and established logistics.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Old school proven vs revolutionary untested - which approach wins?

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Leopard 2A8 represents evolutionary improvement with modular armor and digital systems, while T-14 attempts revolutionary crew protection design. Both emphasize advanced fire control but Leopard has production maturity.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: European precision engineering vs Russian innovation - contrasting military industrial approaches

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Both feature advanced digital systems and crew protection, but Challenger 3 uses proven Challenger 2 hull while T-14 is clean-sheet design. Challenger 3 has realistic production timeline.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Upgrade vs clean-sheet design philosophies in modern MBT development

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Both incorporate advanced APS and digital systems, but K2 has achieved successful production and export sales while T-14 remains limited. K2 emphasizes mobility, T-14 emphasizes protection.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Asian military innovation vs Russian - who actually delivered on next-gen MBT promises?

peer_comparisons.key_differences: Type 99A represents Chinese conventional approach with proven systems, while T-14 shows Russian appetite for radical innovation. Type 99A achieved larger production numbers but less advanced crew protection.
peer_comparisons.video_angle: Two different paths for Russian and Chinese armor modernization
